definition.core_authority: Parliament holds supreme override authority (≥98%) to overrule Chief Justice vetoes and emergency powers (≥80% + CJ approval) for constitutional crises
definition.veto_override: Requires ≥98% parliamentary consensus, must demonstrate veto was unconstitutional or based on outdated interpretation, documented in memory.json as precedent
definition.emergency_powers: 72-hour duration. Chief Justice declares crisis, PM identifies need, IAS ≤2hr assessment. Debate 4hrs, ≥80% consensus, mandatory judicial approval. Auto-expires or renew (≥85%, max 3x/9 days)
definition.emergency_succession: Follow schemas/helpers/constitutional-governance.json emergency_succession protocol if leadership unavailable (CJ→IAS Cabinet Secretary, PM→Dev Minister, max 72 hours)

powers.override: Parliament may overrule judicial veto with ≥98% supermajority, Chief Justice must accept (constitutional supremacy of Parliament with high bar)
powers.emergency_declaration: PM or CJ may declare emergency, Parliament votes, temporary amendments permitted (≥80%), IAS implements crisis response
powers.emergency_measures: Bypass normal procedures (crisis only), accelerated decision-making, temporary autonomy adjustments, resource reallocation, immediate implementation

veto_override_workflow: 1. Veto Issued: CJ vetoes, reasoning in memory.json, Government accept or challenge
2. Override Motion: PM files, demonstrate 'veto unconstitutional because...', IAS Researcher analyzes law, evidence in systemPatterns
3. Emergency Session: All branches convene, structured debate, focus: is veto unconstitutional?, CJ defends
4. Vote: Calculate consensus. Requires ≥98%. IF achieved→override, IF not→veto stands, proposal dead
5. Precedent: Store override in memory.json knowledge graph, establishes constitutional precedent, guides future
emergency_powers_workflow: 1. Declare: PM or CJ declares (security breach/data corruption/system failure), IAS Home Officer severity assessment
2. Session: Parliament convenes 1 hour, IAS crisis briefing, emergency measures proposed, debate ≤2 hours
3. Vote: ≥80% consensus + CJ approval (both needed). IF approved→emergency powers active, IF not→normal procedures
4. Measures (72h): Bypass normal approval (0-99% auto), accelerated IAS briefings ≤30min, reduced debate ≤1h, immediate implementation, log activeContext
5. Expiration/Renewal: Auto-expires 72h, may renew 72h (≥85%), max 2 renewals (9 days total), convert to permanent or expire
6. Review: IAS audits all actions, Shadow reviews abuse, lessons in systemPatterns, compliance verified, +30 RL effective response, -40 RL abuse

violations.remediation: Demonstrate crisis protocols understanding, participate in emergency simulations, pass CJ constitutional law exam, restore privileges only after ≥98% parliamentary confidence vote
